reply:
[[BLOCK:slide11_shape1]]
Zero indicates no variance is explained.  
When a line is fitted to the data, it results in a flat (horizontal) line.  
If the model performs worse than a horizontal line and contradicts the observed data pattern, the adjusted R-squared may become negative.  
This often occurs when there are too many predictors in the model, making the model less effective than having no model at all.  
Metaphorically, this is akin to being ill and taking the wrong medication or a placebo, which can be more harmful than taking no medication.
